description: Herpetofauna (Amphibia & Reptilia) observations from the iNaturalist Open Dataset via Observa

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS total FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring});

SELECT count(*) AS amphibia FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring});

SELECT count(*) AS reptilia FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036) AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring});

SELECT count(DISTINCT t.taxon_id) AS species FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND t.rank = 'species' AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring});

SELECT date_trunc('month', o.observed_on)::timestamptz AS time, count(*) FILTER (WHERE ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) AS "Amphibia", count(*) FILTER (WHERE ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036) AS "Reptilia" FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring}) AND o.observed_on IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(o.observed_on) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

WITH first_seen AS (SELECT CASE WHEN ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978 THEN 'Amphibia' WHEN ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036 THEN 'Reptilia' END AS class, t.taxon_id, min(o.observed_on) AS first_obs FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND t.rank = 'species' AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring}) AND o.observed_on IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(o.observed_on) GROUP BY 1, 2), monthly AS (SELECT date_trunc('month', first_obs)::timestamptz AS month, count(*) FILTER (WHERE class = 'Amphibia') AS new_amphibia, count(*) FILTER (WHERE class = 'Reptilia') AS new_reptilia FROM first_seen GROUP BY 1) SELECT month AS time, sum(new_amphibia) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS "Amphibia", sum(new_reptilia) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS "Reptilia" FROM monthly ORDER BY 1;

SELECT to_char(to_date(EXTRACT(month FROM o.observed_on)::text, 'MM'), 'MM Mon') AS month, count(*) FILTER (WHERE ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) AS "Amphibia", count(*) FILTER (WHERE ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036) AS "Reptilia" FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring}) AND o.observed_on IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Observation Density Map
panels.description: Density grid at 0.5° resolution (~55 km N-S × 45 km E-W at lat 36°, ~2,500 km² per cell). Marker size scales with observations per cell. Cell boundaries nest with the Site x Species Matrix below — each density cell contains exactly 25 SAR cells (5×5 block of 0.1° cells), so a hotspot here corresponds to up to 25 rows in the SAR export.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ST_Y(grid_geom) AS latitude, ST_X(grid_geom) AS longitude, sum(observation_count) AS metric FROM mv_herpetofauna_grid WHERE quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring}) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY metric DESC;

panels.title: Top 15 Families
panels.description: Family-level counts computed by traversing each observation's taxon ancestry and matching family-rank ancestors

targets.rawSql: WITH obs_ancestors AS (SELECT string_to_array(t.ancestry, '/')::int[] AS ancestors FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND t.ancestry IS NOT NULL AND t.ancestry <> '' AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring})) SELECT fam.name AS family, count(*) AS obs_count FROM obs_ancestors oa JOIN taxa fam ON fam.taxon_id = ANY(oa.ancestors) WHERE fam.rank = 'family' GROUP BY fam.name ORDER BY obs_count DESC LIMIT 15;

SELECT t.name AS species, CASE WHEN ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978 THEN 'Amphibia' WHEN ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036 THEN 'Reptilia' END AS class, count(*) AS obs_count, min(o.observed_on) AS first_observed, max(o.observed_on) AS last_observed, count(DISTINCT concat(round(o.latitude::numeric, 1), ',', round(o.longitude::numeric, 1))) AS approx_sites FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND t.rank = 'species' AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring}) AND $__timeFilter(o.observed_on) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY obs_count DESC;

panels.title: Site x Species Matrix (0.1° grid)
panels.description: Site × Species matrix at uniform 0.1° resolution (~11 km N-S × 9 km E-W at lat 36°, ~100 km² per cell). One row per grid cell with herp species richness and per-class breakdown. Useful for: beta-diversity calculations, hotspot identification, mapping species richness, and feeding into species accumulation curves via `vegan::specaccum()`. Cell boundaries nest within the 0.5° Observation Density Map above (25 SxS cells = 1 density map cell). Cells with fewer than 5 total observations are excluded. NOTE: This is NOT a SAR-ready export — uniform-area cells don't have enough area dynamic range to support a meaningful species-area curve fit. For SAR fitting, use the Multi-Scale Species-Area Aggregation panel below.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ST_Y(ST_SnapToGrid(o.geom, 0.1)) AS grid_lat, ST_X(ST_SnapToGrid(o.geom, 0.1)) AS grid_lon, count(DISTINCT t.taxon_id) AS species_richness, count(*) AS total_observations, count(DISTINCT t.taxon_id) FILTER (WHERE ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) AS amphibia_species, count(DISTINCT t.taxon_id) FILTER (WHERE ('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036) AS reptilia_species FROM observations o JOIN taxa t ON o.taxon_id = t.taxon_id WHERE ((('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/20978/%' OR t.taxon_id = 20978) OR (('/' || COALESCE(t.ancestry, '') || '/') LIKE '%/26036/%' OR t.taxon_id = 26036)) AND t.rank = 'species' AND o.quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring}) AND o.geom IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(o.observed_on) GROUP BY 1, 2 HAVING count(*) >= 5 ORDER BY species_richness DESC;

panels.title: Multi-Scale Species-Area Aggregation (SAR-ready)
panels.description: Species-area data ready for fitting with the `sars` R package or similar SAR libraries. Aggregates herp observations across 5 grid resolutions (0.1° / 0.2° / 0.4° / 0.8° / 1.6°), giving a ~256× area dynamic range. Each row is one (scale, cell) with the cell's geodesic area in km² and species counts broken out per taxonomic group: total herps, Amphibia (class), Reptilia (class), Anura (frogs), Caudata (salamanders), Testudines (turtles), and Serpentes (snakes). cell_area_km2 is computed as the geodesic surface area of each cell on the WGS84 ellipsoid via ST_Area(geography), so the values correctly account for Earth curvature (cells at higher latitudes are smaller). Backed by mv_herpetofauna_sar_data which is rebuilt on every import. To fit a SAR for any group in R: `library(sars); df <- read.csv('export.csv'); fit <- sar_power(data.frame(a = df$cell_area_km2, s = df$amphibia_species)); summary(fit)`. Substitute amphibia_species → reptilia_species / anura_species / caudata_species / testudines_species / serpentes_species / total_species for other groups. For multi-model averaging use `sar_average()` instead of `sar_power()`.

targets.rawSql: SELECT scale_deg, ST_Y(grid_geom) AS cell_lat, ST_X(grid_geom) AS cell_lon, cell_area_km2, total_species, amphibia_species, reptilia_species, anura_species, caudata_species, testudines_species, serpentes_species FROM mv_herpetofauna_sar_data WHERE quality_grade IN (${quality:sqlstring}) ORDER BY scale_deg, total_species DESC;

title: Herpetofauna Observations
